steps.title: Identify the starting value
steps.content: The function uses $x$ years after $1998$. At $x=0$, the estimated population is $2{,}000$, so the equation must start with $2{,}000$.

steps.title: Convert the percent increase to a multiplier
steps.content: A $3\%$ yearly increase means each year's population is $100\%+3\%=103\%$ of the previous year's population. As a decimal, the multiplier is $1.03$.

steps.title: Write the exponential model
steps.content: The model is $$p(x)=2{,}000(1.03)^x$$ This has initial value $2{,}000$ and multiplies by $1.03$ each year, so choice C is correct.
